title: Anchor Hocking 5-Ounce Measuring Glass, Small
description:
About this item
Small measuring glass made in the USA
4 measurement markings: Tablespoon, teaspoon, ounces and milliliters
Dishwasher safe
reviews_5s:
Love the measurements on glassThis measuring glass can be extremely helpful when used to capture the espresso dispensed by espresso machine. The ideal measurement for a double shot is 80ML. I used to use a regular measuring cup, but it was hard to read the fine measurements. I even questioned the accuracy, due to measuring cups having a fluted shape, but uniform measuring marks. The measuring cup also had a tendency to disappear, and be put away in some unknown place in the kitchen.

This measuring cup is so small that it obviously belongs with similar sized accessories, so it doesn't disappear. When the espresso is dispensed, it is much easier to tell, even by sight, whether the correct amount has been dispensed. The measuring cup is large enough for 120ML, so if the grind is not adjusted correctly, it doesn't over-flow, like some smaller 100ML measuring cups that were for sale.

There are measurement for teaspoons, tablespoons, cup size, ounces, etc, so you do have to twist the cup around to find the correct measurement, but nothing in life is perfect, that's the price of drinking perfect espresso. At least the cup can have other uses.I love these little things. Bought them to mix pesticide into skin cream to make a lotion to kill scabies. The lotion worked, but not as well as the prescription stuff--of course, try to get prescription stuff when those bugs are eating you at 1:30 a.m., like they seem to love to do.

Nice, because they're glass, and won't absorb the insecticide. Also, with the various volume indicators, good for mixing small amounts of almost anything. I've checked, and the indicators are properly printed on the glass for the volumes they indicate. So, good quality control there. They're tough too. I've dropped one (perhaps two or three different ones) two or three times, with a hard enough impact I feared breakage, which did not occur. Of course, I don't recommend testing them until they break, unless you like that sort of thing, but I do recommend these for accurate, small measuring chores.Replaces inferior productVery goodLike a skillful actor, it always hits its mark. Never makes a fuss about coming out of its quarters in order to do its job. At any hour. Even after a full day of work, it will perform amiably, never forgetting its lines.
The only concern is the tendency that it shares with many performers; it can be a bit sensitive at times and is prone to suffering break-ups.
But when it feels sheltered it looks good, feels good, and executes admirably.I love this for just about everything, and I especially love using this to measure a few tablespoons of liquid. I use it all the time. My only quibble is that it’s pretty easily breakable, especially when your resident dishwasher is also a well-known breaker of things and is not always super gentle in general. So I bought two and keep one squirreled away somewhere safe, and I try to remember to immediately wash and put this away so that he doesn’t have an opportunity to bash it against the inside of the sink AGAIN (he’s broken two already... so I’m on 3 and 4...) But I just love it so much I keep buying it....I had previously bought the knock-off "anchor" version at a Big Lots! store some years ago. It was great, but the red lettering has mostly worn off, making it now a juice glass and not a measuring glass. I just bought this and it seems to be a genuine Anchor Hocking glass. (it was not etched on the bottom, but there was a label on the bottom, also made in USA) Hopefully, the lettering will not wash or wear off like the knock-off. There's seems to be a lot of interchanging and conflating of the two, here and elsewhere.I have just gotten this cup and it appears well made and serviceable. Others have commented that the marks wash off-- I don't know but at the very least the marks are clear and readable unlike other measuring cups I have bought recently. It appears that the newest chinese innovation in reducing manufacturing costs is to print the marks on self adhesive transparent labels and save ink by making the lines and text as thin and small as possible. At the same time as this order I also received a small measuring cup which had marks that are not easily discernible with the naked eye under normal room light. In fact, very close to my face, under the stove hood light, using a MAGNIFYING GLASS they were still hard to distinguish and hard to read. This is not just my opinion but corroborated by others I showed it to also. I have an anchor-hocking (I believe) 8 cup glass where the markings are still legible and EASY TO READ after at least 50 years. I got it from my Mother's kitchen after she passed.Nice size to measure small quantities accurately.
reviews_1s:
For the first time ever, I wish I could give a product zero stars! I looked and looked for this product and decided to take a chance despite all the reviews regarding the measurements washing off in the dishwasher. Well, I'd be perfectly fine if the measurements washed off because they are not accurate!!

I bought this measuring cup to help me with something that I take in 4 oz. dosages. A regular shot glass was a pain because 4 oz. is all the way to the rim (easy to spill). Using a regular measuring cup was a pain because they were too big to tote around with me. I mix up my stuff in a 16 oz. container and then use the measuring cup to pour out 4 oz. four times a day. The first day I used this, I got to the third dosage and realized I had more than half left. Figured I must have poured something wrong the first two times. The second day I used this - same thing, so I pulled out all the measuring cups I have (bigger ones for cooking - several 8 and 16 oz. cups, different brands bought over many years). First I checked 16 ounces - all the big measuring cups matched each other. Then I checked 8 oz. - all the big measuring cups matched each other. Then I check 4 oz. on this Anchor Hocking measuring glass against the big measuring cups - all matched EXCEPT this cup. What shows as 4 oz. on all the other measuring cups shows as 4.5 oz. on this measuring glass. What a waste of money. The whole point is to measure accurately and it does not.I have a pair of these bought some time back and I just noticed that they are both off by about 20-25% (depending on scale) as measured against a graduated cylinder. For something whose only purpose in life is to measure it really is thoroughly inadequate. Not only that, but it measures *high* so I have gotten short-changed on any number of cocktails which makes me sad.

The photo shows the two Anchor glasses plus a Measure-n-Pour measuring glass, each filled with 50ml of water from a graduated cylinder.Out of the box the measurements were at an angle and the glass (albeit with stickers on it) looks like it has been resold. The markings were faded which I wouldn't expect from a new glass. The biggest issue with this was the measurement itself; it wouldn't be practical to tilt my glass every time I needed to measure something.

This shouldn't be a prime item, it can't be returned even if it is defective. I had to get a refund through Amazon, but they refused to take the item back, so I had to throw the glass away. Overall this was a weird purchase... I wish there were a way to block vendors like this from our profiles.This is supposed to be a precision tool, so you can get a clear measurement down to a TBSP or TSP. So imagine my surprise when all of the measurements on this little shot glass were off! We tested it against 2 other shot-glass-style measuring cups as well as traditional spoons in our kitchen, and the measurements on this were nowhere near correct. More: they weren't even consistently off. The 1 TBSP line was at 1.5 tbsp. The 2 TBSP line was at 2.5 TBSP. The 3 TBSP line was at around 4 TBSP. You could see just by looking at it that the lines we unevenly spaced (and I don't mean "slowly getting closer together as the glass widens towards the top").

We sent ours back. Don't waste your money.This item arrived cracked and when I attempted to start a return I found it was “not returnable.” No way to request a replacement for the broken item. Would give this a 0 if I could.I don't like to complain, but item came broken, and when I tap on the link to return/replace it says item is ineligible for return. Packing was super crappy with the glass stuck in the bottom corner of the cardboard box and air packs all to one side. To compare, I also just received a measuring shot glass, that came paper wrapped, then bubble wrapped, and placed in a bubble envelope - arrived perfectly fine.I received this product in shattered pieces. I tried to return on Amazon, which directed me to the manufacturer. After finally reaching someone at Anchor Hocking, they said they’d send a replacement. To date, I have received nothing....not very happy with Amazon or Anchor Hocking right now....only a $6 item, but seems like I should have received a replacement from Amazon since AH is a National brand.Our order was from Anchor Hocking, which we have used for years. Ordered two of the 5 ounce measuring cups. Both are defective. The glass inside bottom for each is not level. 1 tbsp. of water measures almost 2 ounces in each of the cups. This is unacceptable, and very disappointing. Sad for Anchor Hocking.If you don't have excellent eyes, beware of purchasing this. The measurements are difficult to read because the color is lighter than sis shown in the product picture. On another note, they slapped a sticker on the side that's very difficult to remove all the sticky. Why do they do that?! I wouldn't have purchased this product if i'd have known. I'll probably end up tossing and buying a different one.GARBAGE! don't waste your money. In less than a month all of the markings are gone. washed rite off in my hands. I now have a glass cup basically and I bought 2 of them!!!! that does me no good!
I will not buy anchor products again, this is the second thing I have had problems with from them within weeks of buying them.
anchor = garbage! and their cust service is awful! I contacted them on the other item I bought, and what a joke!
Go Pyrex all the way!!!
